channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Arizona recreation area closed after hiker death and nearly 3 dozen rescues in just 2 days
item.description: Arizona’s Lake Mead National Recreation Area have closed multiple trails due to dangerously high temperatures and the death of one hiker and rescue of 33 others over two days.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Viral CIA file about aliens attacking soldiers takes off with UFO intrigue
item.description: A Soviet-era document published on the CIA's website describes a retaliatory attack by aliens after soldiers shot down a UFO nearly 30 years ago.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ecuador's incumbent president and a leftist lawyer again vie for the top job in a runoff election
item.description: QUITO, Ecuador (AP) — A conservative young millionaire who is Ecuador 's incumbent president and a leftist lawyer are again the choices as Ecuadorians head to the polls on Sunday in the country’s second presidential runoff election in less than two years.
item.dc:creator: GONZALO SOLANO and REGINA GARCIA CANO, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Central Myanmar shaken by new quake in one of the biggest aftershocks since March 28 temblor
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — A magnitude 5.5 earthquake struck on Sunday morning near Meiktila, a small city in central Myanmar, according to the U.S. Geological Survey.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Western Michigan wins its 1st Frozen Four hockey title, beating Boston University 6-2
item.description: ST. LOUIS (AP) — Owen Michaels didn’t hesitate after watching his shot slide into the open net with more than two minutes left to essentially seal Western Michigan’s first Frozen Four title in its first championship game.
item.dc:creator: JOHN WAWROW, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Russia and Ukraine accuse each other of failing to pause strikes after US envoy leaves Moscow
item.description: Russia and Ukraine’s top diplomats on Saturday used a high-level conference in Turkey to once again trade accusations of violating a tentative U.S.-brokered deal to pause strikes on energy infrastructure, underscoring the challenges of negotiating an end to the 3-year-old war.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Wisconsin teen charged in shooting death of mother, stepfather found decomposed in their home
item.description: A 17-year-old Wisconsin boy is accused of killing his parents and leaving their bodies to rot in their home for weeks before fleeing the state in their SUV.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fan reaches into Mike Trout's glove to snatch a catch from Angels outfielder
item.description: A fan grabbed the ball out of Mike Trout's glove after the Los Angeles Angels star reached into the right field stands Saturday night to make what appeared to be a great catch against the Houston Astros
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: NTSB finds tour helicopter that crashed into Hudson River was not equipped with any flight recorders
item.description: The National Transportation Safety Board (NTSB) confirmed the tour helicopter that fatally crashed into the Hudson River on Thursday did not have any flight records.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What we know about the New York City helicopter crash that killed 6 people
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A New York City sightseeing helicopter broke apart and crashed into the Hudson River near the New Jersey shoreline, killing the pilot and a Spanish family of five who were on board.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pet food recalled after testing positive for salmonella and listeria: FDA
item.description: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration has announced a voluntary recall of certain puppy and kitten foods.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Langenfeld, KTLA 5

item.title: Intellectually disabled teen shot by Idaho police dies after being removed from life support
item.description: BOISE, Idaho (AP) — An autistic, nonverbal teenage boy who was shot repeatedly by Idaho police from the other side of a chain-link fence while he was holding a knife died Saturday after being removed from life support, his family said.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA BELLISLE and REBECCA BOONE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: US won't say whether it's facilitating return of mistakenly deported man, despite judge's order
item.description: The Trump administration confirmed to a federal judge Saturday that a Maryland man who was mistakenly deported last month remains confined in a notorious prison in El Salvador.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Dominican club collapse death toll rises to 226 after woman pulled from rubble dies in hospital
item.description: SANTO DOMINGO, Dominican Republic (AP) — A woman hospitalized after being rescued from the rubble of a roof collapse at a popular nightclub in the Dominican Republic died on Saturday, raising the death toll to 226, health officials said.
item.dc:creator: MARTÍN ADAMES, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Serbia's populist president demands order after months of anti-corruption protests shaking his rule
item.description: BELGRADE, Serbia (AP) — Serbia's populist President Aleksandar Vucic on Saturday demanded that authorities restore “order and peace” in the Balkan country following months of anti-corruption protests that have shaken his firm grip on power.
item.dc:creator: JOVANA GEC, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Kansas man wrongly accused in Chiefs parade shooting found dead
item.description: An Olathe, Kansas man who was wrongly accused of being a "shooter" and "illegal alien" at the Chiefs Super Bowl rally in 2024 has died.
item.dc:creator: Brian Dulle, Fox 5

item.title: LDS Church loses lawsuit over payment of sexual abuse settlements
item.description: The Utah District Court has ruled against the Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints in a lawsuit filed over the payment of sex abuse settlements.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Bittan, ABC 4

item.title: Judge relaxes ban on DOGE access to sensitive US Treasury information
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A New York judge has relaxed a ban she’d put on Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency preventing it from accessing sensitive Treasury Department information related to millions of Americans. Judge Jeannette A.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A bucket of beans and someone's pet turtle: Uber reveals 'most unique' items passengers left behind
item.description: It's no surprise that phones, wallets and keys are often left behind in rideshare vehicles. But a urinal or a taxidermied rabbit? Those are slightly more rare.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, ABC 4

item.title: US may already be in recession, says BlackRock CEO
item.description: BlackRock CEO Larry Fink said during a recent interview that the United States may already be in a recession or getting very close to one as a result of President Trump’s sweeping tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump exempts phones, computers, other electronics from reciprocal tariffs
item.description: The Trump administration is exempting electronics such as phones, computers and other electronics from the "reciprocal" tariffs imposed on other nations, including China.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Trump announces $600 million in new deals with five law firms
item.description: The deals bring to nine the number of firms that have struck agreements with Trump in the wake of numerous actions targeting major law offices.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KRON4

item.title: Amateur golfer urinates in creek at Masters Tournament, gets round of applause
item.description: U.S. Amateur champion Jose Luis Ballester had to take a different kind of relief Thursday at the Masters, just not the kind found in the Rules of Golf.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: NYC sightseeing helicopter plunges into river, killing 6 including Spanish family and pilot
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A New York City sightseeing helicopter broke apart in midair Thursday and crashed upside-down into the Hudson River, killing the pilot and a family of five Spanish tourists in the latest U.S. aviation disaster, officials said.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, JENNIFER PELTZ and TED SHAFFREY, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Weapons, ammo stopped from heading into Mexico
item.description: A cache of weapons and hundreds of rounds of ammunition have been seized from vehicles recently at the Del Rio Port of Entry, U.S. Customs and Border Protection says.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: No, Casey Anthony did not open a Branson daycare
item.description: Google Trends showed a variety of topics being searched in Missouri on Thursday morning---sports, weather, the lottery, and one specific question: Did Casey Anthony open a daycare in Branson, Missouri?
item.dc:creator: Megan Mueller, Fox 5

item.title: Supreme Court rules Trump administration must 'facilitate’ return of mistakenly deported man
item.description: The court’s emergency ruling instructs the lower judge to clarify the language of her order but said she acted properly in ensuring that Kilmar Abrego Garcia’s case is handled “as it would have been had he not been improperly sent to El Salvador.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, WGN 9

item.title: Judge will halt Trump administration from ending humanitarian parole for people from four countries
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — A federal judge said Thursday that she will prevent the Trump administration from ordering hundreds of thousands of Cubans, Haitians, Nicaraguans and Venezuelans with temporary legal status to leave the country later this month.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Pressed for evidence against Mahmoud Khalil, government cites its power to deport people for beliefs
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Facing a deadline from an immigration judge to turn over evidence for its attempted deportation of Columbia University activist Mahmoud Khalil, the federal government has instead submitted a brief memo, signed by Secretary of State Marco Rubio, citing the Trump administration’s autho
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Report: Drug lord linked to DEA agent’s death freed
item.description: One of the three leaders of the Guadalajara cartel linked to the torture-slaying of Drug Enforcement Administration Agent Enrique “Kiki” Camarena in 1985 has been released in Mexico, according to news reports.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, NewsNation

item.title: US stocks dive as euphoria on Wall Street reverts to fear about US-China trade war
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — U.S. stocks dove Thursday and surrendered a chunk of their historic gains from the day before as President Donald Trump’s trade war continues to threaten the economy.
item.dc:creator: STAN CHOE, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Hundreds of students, dozens of colleges hit by Trump's visa purge: What to know
item.description: Hundreds of foreign students at dozens of colleges across the country have had their higher education experience turned upside down as the Trump administration has expanded its immigration crackdown beyond those involved in the pro-Palestinian protests.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KRON4

item.title: Trump reverses Biden rule restricting water usage for showerheads and other home appliances
item.description: President Donald Trump has long complained about modern rules that limit water flow for showerheads, making it harder for him to wash his "beautiful hair.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Daly, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Egg prices jump to record high despite Trump's predictions and bird flu slowing
item.description: The increase reported Thursday in the Consumer Price Index means consumers and businesses that rely on eggs should not anticipate immediate relief.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Sweepstakes giant Publishers Clearing House files for bankruptcy
item.description: Publishers Clearing House, famous for its big checks and sweepstakes, files for Chapter 11 bankruptcy as it shifts to digital advertising.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, Fox 5

item.title: Ballerina Ksenia Karelina freed from Russian custody in prisoner exchange
item.description: Ksenia Karelina, a U.S.-Russian dual national who was detained last year on treason charges, was released from Russia in the latest prisoner exchange, according to the State Department.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: At least 124 dead after roof collapse at Dominican club, officials scramble to ID victims
item.description: Rescue crews from Puerto Rico and Israel arrived Wednesday to help with the search. Officials said crews used sonar to detect what could be one person still breathing under the rubble.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5
